strengths: Nearly 20 years of hands-on development across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded — far exceeding the 6+ year requirement — with recent full-stack TypeScript/Node.js/React product work at Optilyz and re:cinq
Direct, current AI agent development experience at re:cinq, building MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows — a rare, explicitly required skill for this role

Proven engineering craftsmanship: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development at RIDE, authored testing tools (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and taught testing/TDD courses — strong match for code review, quality, and process-improvement responsibilities
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience listed, and much of his frontend history is Ember.js rather than React (though React was used at Optilyz)
Kotlin is absent from his skills and Java experience appears dated (Altom, 2016–2018), leaving the secondary JVM stack only partially covered
Vitest/Playwright and specific cloud-platform depth beyond AWS CDK and self-hosted Kubernetes are not explicitly evidenced
response.justification: James is a seasoned full-stack engineer whose experience substantially exceeds the role's bar: he has shipped TypeScript/Node.js/React/MongoDB product features at scale (Optilyz's direct-mail platform), practiced disciplined engineering (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) at RIDE, and operated a Kubernetes-deployed open-source platform he founded. Most decisively, his current role at re:cinq involves building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — the exact 'AI agent development' requirement most candidates lack. His polyglot background (TypeScript, Java, C/C++, D, Swift), database and Docker experience, security-adjacent systems work, and long record of teaching and community talks map directly onto the role's architecture, code-review, and collaboration responsibilities. Gaps like Nest.js and Kotlin are framework-level details a developer of his demonstrated adaptability — having repeatedly picked up new stacks, including taking over an iOS app that reached the US top 10 — can be expected to close quickly.
